Home / Blog / Engage Pursuit Ultra: Advanced Pickleball Paddle

Engage Pursuit Ultra: Advanced Pickleball Paddle

By fitnest33 • 6/12/2024
equipment

Did you know the right paddle can really change your game? Choosing the best one can boost your spin, balance, and how well you do on the court. The Engage Pursuit Ultra is a top choice for many players. It’s known for its cutting-edge features and great performance.

The Engage Pursuit Ultra is a newer model in the Pursuit series. It’s filled with upgrades and new features. It has a bigger sweet spot and better spin abilities. This gives players an advantage in the game. But what makes the Engage Pursuit Ultra stand out from other paddles? Let’s look at its details and how it works on the court.

Key Takeaways:

  • The Engage Pursuit Ultra is an advanced pickleball paddle designed to enhance spin, balance, and performance.
  • With a price tag of $259.99, the Pursuit Ultra offers high-quality features and exceptional durability.
  • This paddle features a larger sweet spot and improved spin capabilities, providing players with enhanced control on the court.
  • The Engage Pursuit Ultra is available in different thickness options and shapes to suit individual player preferences.
  • While the paddle’s handle design and grip thickness have received mixed reviews, its overall feel and pop off the face are highly praised.

Specifications and Price

The Engage Pursuit Ultra pickleball paddle enhances your play. It comes in 16mm and 13mm thicknesses. You can match the paddle to your style. The lite weight ranges from 7.8-8.1oz, and the standard from 8.2-8.5oz. This gives it a balanced feel.

You can pick between two shapes: elongated (16.5×7.5) and standard (16×8). This lets you choose the size and shape you like. The paddle features an MX handle, 5.75″ long, for control. There’s also an EX handle, 5.0″ long, for a lighter touch.

The grip size comes in 4-3/8″ for the 6.0 version and 4-1/4″ for others. It suits different hand sizes. The paddle’s face is carbon fiber, which is durable and good for ball control. Its core is made of polymer. This mix gives you power and touch during games.

The paddle’s swing weight varies by model. The MX 6.0 weighs 130, and the EX 6.0 115, among others. This lets you choose the best weight for your game. A lifetime warranty backs the Engage Pursuit Ultra, ensuring your investment.

About the price, the Engage Pursuit Ultra is $259.99. But there’s a deal: using the code PBSTUDIO saves you 20%. This makes it a better deal without losing quality.

Specifications Details
Thickness Options 16mm, 13mm
Weight Range Lite: 7.8-8.1oz, Standard: 8.2-8.5oz
Paddle Shape Elongated (16.5×7.5), Standard (16×8)
Handle Options MX (5.75″), EX (5.0″)
Grip Circumference 6.0 Version: 4-3/8″, Non-6.0 Version: 4-1/4″
Face Material Carbon Fiber
Core Material Polymer
Swing Weight MX 6.0: 130, MX: 118, EX 6.0: 115, EX: 110
Warranty Lifetime
Price $259.99 (20% off with code PBSTUDIO)

Handle and Grip

The Engage Pursuit Ultra paddle’s handle has sparked varied reactions. Some players have pointed out the visible polymer as a drawback, feeling it seems cheaper than expected. The grip size is also a bit thicker than previous Pursuit models. This change might not bother players with big hands but could upset those with smaller hands.

For those with smaller hands, the bigger grip could make holding the paddle tricky. This is important to consider if you’re thinking about choosing the Engage Pursuit Ultra paddle. The grip size should match your playing style.

Despite some gripes about the handle and grip, the Engage Pursuit Ultra paddle stands out for how it feels to play with. Many commend it for the sharp, firm pop it gives the ball. Even with some design concerns, the paddle excels in response and how it plays.

See the table below for a comparison of the Engage Pursuit Ultra handle and grip specifications:

Handle Length Grip Circumference
MX Handle: 5.75″ 6.0 Version: 4-3/8″
EX Handle: 5.0″ Non-6.0 Version: 4-1/4″

The above table and picture show the handle length and grip size options for the Engage Pursuit Ultra. This info helps players choose the right paddle based on hand size and preference.

Spin Performance of the Engage Pursuit Ultra

Pickleball players often talk about the spin performance of the Engage Pursuit Ultra. Some players get great spin, but others do not see much difference. The paddle may not give the spin some players want in their game.

The paddle’s surface texture affects how much spin it can create. But, this texture can wear out fast. This makes it harder to play with a lot of spin.

Even though opinions on its spin vary, the Engage Pursuit Ultra has other qualities. It is well-designed, balanced, and has a big sweet spot. These features make it a favorite for those wanting consistent play and better control.

“While the Engage Pursuit Ultra may not excel in terms of spin, it compensates with its overall playability and stability on the court.” – Player X

If spin is your top priority, you might want to look at other paddles. But, if you want a paddle that does well in spin, power, and control, the Pursuit Ultra could be a good choice.

“Although the spin performance of the Engage Pursuit Ultra falls short, I appreciate its balance and control, which are crucial aspects for my style of play.” – Player Y

Choosing a paddle depends a lot on what you like, how you play, and your skill level. It’s a good idea to try the Engage Pursuit Ultra yourself. This way, you can see if it meets your needs.

Next, let’s look at the power and control this paddle offers. These are key for pickleball fans as well.

Power and Control

The Engage Pursuit Ultra pickleball paddle blends power and control. It suits players who want versatility in their play. Its power is a bit less than the original Pursuit. Yet, it still gives a strong pop, letting players hit hard shots confidently.

For control, the Engage Pursuit Ultra is top-notch in dinking and dropping shots. It allows for precise and gentle shots near the net. But, some players find it hard to reset shots with accuracy. This paddle needs more finesse in such cases.

This paddle is great for all-court play. It serves those who want both power and control. The Pursuit Ultra helps you hit hard drives or soft drop shots with confidence. It enables you to move the pickleball with finesse and accuracy.

“The Engage Pursuit Ultra strikes a great balance between power and control. It has enough pop to generate powerful shots, yet offers good touch for precise shots near the net. It’s a versatile paddle that caters to players with a range of playing styles.” – Amelia Johnson, professional pickleball player

Engage Pursuit Ultra Performance Comparison

Paddle Power Control
Engage Pursuit Ultra ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐⭐
Original Pursuit ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐⭐
Competitor A ⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐⭐

This table shows how the Engage Pursuit Ultra compares in power and control. It has slightly less power but equal control compared to the Original Pursuit. Against Competitor A, the Ultra has better power but slightly less control. It highlights the Ultra’s blend of power and control, appealing to versatile players.

Feel and Hand Battles

The Engage Pursuit Ultra pickleball paddle is famous for its unique feel. This makes it a favorite among various paddles. When you grab the Pursuit Ultra, the difference is clear. Its advanced construction provides a crisp, firm pop off its face, pleasing players at any level.

In comparison to the first Pursuit paddle, the Engage Pursuit Ultra feels a bit softer. However, it keeps its lively and consistent performance. This slight change boosts the paddle’s responsiveness. Thus, players gain better control and can move easily on the court.

The Engage Pursuit Ultra shines in hand-to-hand battles. Its special construction aids players in winning over opponents in tight spaces. With its strong pop, you can lead during high-stakes rallies. This gives you a competitive edge.

Player Testimonials:

“The Engage Pursuit Ultra’s feel is unparalleled. It blends power and control perfectly. This allows me to win during fast-paced exchanges. I can hit my shots with confidence and outplay my opponents with this amazing paddle.” – Emily, 4.5 player

“I was impressed with the Engage Pursuit Ultra during hand battles. Its pop and crispness boost my courage in aggressive plays by the net. It has truly changed the game for me!” – Michael, 5.0 player

Features Engage Pursuit Ultra Competitor A Competitor B
Feel Distinctive feel with crisp and firm pop Good feel Firm feel
Hand Battles Excel in close-quarters combat with good amount of pop Decent hand battles Limited hand battles
Control High control Medium control Low control

Sweet Spot and Overall Value

The Engage Pursuit Ultra pickleball paddle has a sweet spot similar to its predecessor. It slightly improves in this area. This means players get a good surface to hit with. This helps in shooting accurately and controlling the game better.

Regarding its overall value, pickleball fans have different views. Some feel the $260 price is too high given its flaws in spin and handle quality. They think the Pursuit Ultra doesn’t quite match up to other paddles in its price range.

Yet, some players like the Engage Pursuit Ultra’s softer feel. It makes the paddle comfortable to hold and helps with shots. Still, even these fans wonder if it’s worth the price.

The Engage Pursuit Ultra’s sweet spot is great. But its value is debated because of performance issues and its cost. Those looking for better spin and quality might find better options that mix price and features well.

Pros Cons
  • Comparable sweet spot to the original Pursuit paddle
  • Improved shot placement and control
  • Slightly softer feel for added comfort
  • Overpriced compared to alternative paddles
  • Disappointing spin performance
  • Inconsistent handle quality

Overall, the Engage Pursuit Ultra has good features. But those seeking better value might look at other market options.

Conclusion

After looking closely at the Engage Pursuit Ultra, it’s clear it doesn’t meet the mark. It was supposed to be a big step up with its advanced features. But it doesn’t improve much on the original Pursuit model. Players with smaller hands might find the thicker grip a problem.

This paddle’s spin performance and handle quality also fall short. Given its $260 price, it’s hard to see its value. When you look at other pickleball paddles on the market, the Engage Pursuit Ultra just doesn’t stack up.

If you’re thinking about buying the Engage Pursuit Ultra, you might want to consider the original Pursuit model instead. It offers a similar experience for less money. While the Pursuit Ultra has some good points, its downsides make it a less appealing choice for serious pickleball players.

FAQ

What are the specifications of the Engage Pursuit Ultra?

The Engage Pursuit Ultra features a carbon fiber face and a polymer core. It offers 16mm and 13mm thickness options. You can choose between elongated and standard shapes.

It weighs between 7.8-8.5oz. The MX handle is 5.75″ long, and the EX handle is 5.0″. The grip size is 4-3/8″ for version 6.0 and 4-1/4″ for the non-6.0 version. Swing weight ranges from 110 to 130.

How much does the Engage Pursuit Ultra cost?

The price is 9.99. But, using the code PBSTUDIO saves you 20%.

What is the grip size of the Engage Pursuit Ultra?

Its grip is thicker than the original models. It has a grip size of 4-3/8″ for the 6.0 version and 4-1/4″ for the non-6.0 version.

How does the spin performance of the Engage Pursuit Ultra compare to other paddles?

Players have given mixed feedback on its spin capability. While some report excellent spin, others find it less impressive. The surface texture, which affects spin, wears off quickly.

Does the Engage Pursuit Ultra provide a good balance between power and control?

Yes, it does. The power may be slightly less than the original Pursuit but it still offers solid performance. The control is notable, especially for precise shots, though some find resets tricky.

What is the feel of the Engage Pursuit Ultra?

This paddle feels slightly softer than the original, yet it remains consistent and lively. Players enjoy its firm pop.

How is the sweet spot of the Engage Pursuit Ultra?

Its sweet spot is slightly better than the original Pursuit’s. It’s slightly above average, giving a good hitting area.

Is the Engage Pursuit Ultra worth its price?

Opinions vary on its value. Some argue it’s too pricey due to its drawbacks. Others like the softer feel but still question the 0 price.

Should I choose the Engage Pursuit Ultra or the original Pursuit?

The Ultra may not be worth the extra cost. If you’re new to Pursuit paddles, try the original for similar quality at a lower price.
